title: Placeholder Image Generator
shortDescription: Create placeholder images for UI mocks, blog covers, OG image drafts and design systems.
metaTitle: Placeholder Image Generator | Create Quick Mockup Images
metaDescription: Generate placeholder images for UI mockups, blog covers, social previews and design systems in your browser.
intro: Create clean placeholder images instantly for wireframes, blog headers, social previews and design work. Set the size, generate the image in your browser and download it right away.
overview: This client-side placeholder image generator uses canvas to create simple, fast placeholder graphics without uploading files. It is useful for mockups, content drafts and design systems when you need a temporary image with the right dimensions.
howItWorks: Enter the desired width and height.
Choose optional styling or text settings if available.
Generate the placeholder image in your browser.
Preview the result and download it as needed.

examples.title: Blog header draft
examples.output: Ready-to-use placeholder image for a cover or social share preview.
examples.description: Useful for article visuals before final artwork is available.

examples.title: Product mockup
examples.output: Simple placeholder graphic for interface layouts.
examples.description: Helps keep spacing and composition consistent during design.

faq.question: What is a placeholder image generator used for?
faq.answer: It creates temporary images for layouts, drafts and prototypes when the final visual is not ready.

faq.question: Can I use it without uploading a file?
faq.answer: Yes. It generates the image locally in your browser, so no upload is needed.

faq.question: What sizes can I create?
faq.answer: You can create any common custom size for banners, thumbnails, cards and social previews.

faq.question: Is the generated image downloaded automatically?
faq.answer: No. You preview it first and then download it when you are ready.

seo.title: Placeholder Image Generator
